gloss.en: very cold; covered with ice

words.example: The roads were icy this morning.

gloss.en: a document that shows who you are

words.example: You must carry ID at all times.

gloss.en: perfect; most suitable

words.example: This is an ideal place for a picnic.

gloss.en: in a perfect or most suitable way

words.example: Ideally, we would start the work today.

gloss.en: exactly the same in every detail

words.example: The two sisters wear identical dresses.

gloss.en: to recognize and name who or what something is

words.example: She was able to identify her attacker.

gloss.en: not working or being used; lazy

words.example: The machines stood idle all day.

gloss.en: to admire or love someone very much

words.example: Many fans idolise the young singer.

gloss.en: to admire or love someone very much

words.example: The boy seemed to idolize his older brother.

gloss.en: not knowing about something

words.example: They were ignorant of the danger.

gloss.en: a false idea or belief about something

words.example: She is under the illusion that she will get the job.

gloss.en: to use pictures in a book; to explain with examples

words.example: She illustrated her own books.

gloss.en: a drawing or picture in a book

words.example: The book has fifty full-colour illustrations.

gloss.en: the ability to create pictures in your mind

words.example: The child has a vivid imagination.

gloss.en: a copy of something; the act of copying

words.example: This bag is a cheap imitation of a designer one.

gloss.en: to put deep into a liquid; to involve deeply

words.example: She likes to immerse herself in a good book.

gloss.en: a person who comes to live in another country

words.example: Many immigrants came from other European countries.

gloss.en: the act of coming to live in another country

words.example: The government wants to control illegal immigration.

gloss.en: not following accepted moral rules

words.example: Many people think it is immoral to lie.

gloss.en: living or lasting forever

words.example: In the story, the gods are immortal.

gloss.en: the state of living forever

words.example: Many myths talk about the search for immortality.

gloss.en: protected against a disease or effect
gloss.zh: 有免疫力的；不受影响的

words.example: Adults are often immune to this illness.

gloss.en: fair; not supporting one side

words.example: A judge must be impartial at all times.

gloss.en: annoyed at having to wait

words.example: After waiting so long, I was getting impatient.

gloss.en: in an impatient way; without wanting to wait

words.example: She waited impatiently for the bus.

gloss.en: something that is very important to do

words.example: Reducing waste is a clear imperative for the company.

gloss.en: not lasting forever; temporary

words.example: All these things in life are impermanent.

gloss.en: to put a decision or plan into action

words.example: The school will implement the new rules next year.

gloss.en: a possible effect or result of an action

words.example: They failed to consider the wider implications of their actions.

gloss.en: to suggest something without saying it directly

words.example: Are you implying that I am wrong?

gloss.en: not polite; rude

words.example: It is impolite to interrupt other people.

gloss.en: in a rude or impolite way

words.example: He spoke impolitely to the waiter.

gloss.en: to bring goods into a country from abroad

words.example: They import a lot of food from abroad.

gloss.en: to officially introduce a rule, tax, or punishment

words.example: The UN decided to impose sanctions on the country.

gloss.en: something that cannot happen or be done

words.example: Finishing the work in one day is an impossibility.

gloss.en: not sensible or useful in practice

words.example: Her plan sounds nice but it is impractical.

gloss.en: to make someone admire you or something
gloss.zh: 使钦佩；给…留下深刻印象

words.example: None of the candidates impressed us.

gloss.en: admiring someone or something as very good

words.example: I must admit I am impressed.

gloss.en: a style of painting using light and colour

words.example: The museum has many works of impressionism.

gloss.en: an artist who paints in the impressionist style

words.example: Monet was a famous impressionist.

gloss.en: not likely to be true or to happen

words.example: It is improbable that he will arrive on time.

gloss.en: in a wrong or unsuitable way

words.example: The form was filled in improperly.

gloss.en: made better than before

words.example: The new model has an improved battery.

gloss.en: a sudden strong wish to do something

words.example: She bought the dress on an impulse.

gloss.en: acting suddenly without thinking first

words.example: He is too impulsive when he spends money.

gloss.en: not correct or exact

words.example: The report contained some inaccurate figures.

gloss.en: not alive; not a living thing

words.example: A rock is an inanimate object.

gloss.en: a quality you have from birth

words.example: She has an inborn talent for music.

gloss.en: a substance that gives a sweet smell when burned

words.example: They burned incense in the temple.

gloss.en: something that encourages you to do something

words.example: There is no incentive for people to save fuel.

gloss.en: a unit of length equal to 2.54 centimetres

words.example: She is a few inches taller than me.

gloss.en: something that happens, often unusual or unpleasant

words.example: His bad behaviour was just an isolated incident.

gloss.en: the money a person or country earns

words.example: Average household income fell slightly.

gloss.en: to include something as part of a whole

words.example: The new car design incorporates all the latest safety features.

gloss.en: not accurate or true

words.example: You gave an incorrect answer.

gloss.en: more and more all the time

words.example: Tourism is playing an increasingly important role here.

gloss.en: that cannot be cured

words.example: Sadly, the disease is incurable.

gloss.en: very grateful to someone for help

words.example: I am deeply indebted to my parents.

gloss.en: to start a line of text away from the margin

words.example: You should indent the first line of each paragraph.

gloss.en: freedom from control by others

words.example: Cuba gained independence from Spain in 1898.

gloss.en: in a way that is free from others' control

words.example: The children worked independently on their projects.

gloss.en: too extreme or strong to describe

words.example: The view from the top was indescribable.

gloss.en: a list of topics at the end of a book

words.example: Look it up in the index.

gloss.en: a sign that shows what is happening

words.example: They gave no indication of how the work should be done.

gloss.en: not interested in or caring about something

words.example: He seemed indifferent to her feelings.

gloss.en: a situation that makes you feel ashamed

words.example: He suffered the indignity of being laughed at.

gloss.en: too important to manage without

words.example: A good map is indispensable when travelling.

gloss.en: allowing someone to do or have what they want

words.example: His indulgent parents gave him everything.

gloss.en: connected with industry

words.example: The workers were involved in an industrial dispute.

gloss.en: to develop industries in a country or area

words.example: The country began to industrialise in the last century.

gloss.en: to develop industries in a country or area

words.example: Many nations rushed to industrialize after the war.

gloss.en: not producing the result you want

words.example: The new medicine proved ineffective.

gloss.en: not moving or not chemically active

words.example: The patient lay inert on the bed.

gloss.en: that you cannot avoid or prevent

words.example: It was an inevitable consequence of the decision.

gloss.en: in a way that is certain to happen

words.example: Inevitably, the press exaggerated the story.

gloss.en: having little knowledge or skill at something

words.example: The team is young and inexperienced.

gloss.en: affected by disease or harmful germs

words.example: The wound became infected.

gloss.en: the process of getting or causing a disease

words.example: Wash your hands to prevent infection.

gloss.en: a disease that can spread to others

words.example: The flu is highly infectious.

gloss.en: to decide something is true from information

words.example: Much of the meaning must be inferred from the context.

gloss.en: a conclusion you reach from information

words.example: What inference can we draw from these results?

gloss.en: redness, heat, and swelling in the body

words.example: The cream reduces inflammation of the skin.

gloss.en: causing anger; causing swelling in the body

words.example: He made some inflammatory remarks about the war.

gloss.en: to fill with air or gas; to make larger

words.example: Use a pump to inflate the tyre.

gloss.en: a general rise in prices over time

words.example: The government continued the fight against rising inflation.

gloss.en: a common illness like a bad cold; the flu

words.example: Many workers were off sick with influenza.

gloss.en: information

words.example: For more info, click here.

gloss.en: to tell someone about something officially

words.example: Please inform the public about the new rules.

gloss.en: relaxed and friendly; not strict or official

words.example: The party had an informal atmosphere.

gloss.en: basic systems like roads, power, and water

words.example: The country needs to improve its infrastructure.

gloss.en: to break a law or limit someone's rights

words.example: The new law may infringe our basic rights.

gloss.en: the act of breaking a rule or right

words.example: Copying the song is an infringement of copyright.

gloss.en: to live in a particular place

words.example: Few animals inhabit the desert.

gloss.en: a person or animal living in a place

words.example: He is the oldest inhabitant of the village.

gloss.en: to receive money or property when someone dies

words.example: She inherited a fortune from her father.

gloss.en: happening at the beginning; first

words.example: There is an initial payment of sixty pounds.

gloss.en: at the beginning

words.example: Initially, the system worked well.

gloss.en: a new plan to solve a problem

words.example: They launched a United Nations peace initiative.

gloss.en: physically hurt; having an injury

words.example: His injured leg prevented him from walking.

gloss.en: coloured liquid for writing and printing

words.example: The letter was written in ink.

gloss.en: a quality you are born with

words.example: She has an innate sense of rhythm.

gloss.en: inside; near the centre of a place

words.example: The hotel has a quiet inner courtyard.

gloss.en: in a way that shows no guilt or harm

words.example: The child smiled innocently at the teacher.

gloss.en: the introduction of new ideas or methods

words.example: We live in an age of technological innovation.

gloss.en: introducing or using new ideas

words.example: There will be a prize for the most innovative design.

gloss.en: ideas, time, or effort put into work

words.example: I'd appreciate your input on this.

gloss.en: to ask for information

words.example: I called to inquire about the job.

gloss.en: an official process to find out information

words.example: The police began a murder inquiry.

gloss.en: so close they cannot be separated

words.example: The two friends are inseparable.

gloss.en: to put something into something else

words.example: Insert coins into the slot and press for a ticket.

gloss.en: forming the inner part; not outside

words.example: The inside pages of a newspaper have the news.

gloss.en: a clear understanding of what something is like

words.example: There are many valuable insights in her book.

gloss.en: to firmly demand or say something

words.example: I didn't really want to go but he insisted.

gloss.en: a police officer of middle rank; an examiner

words.example: The inspector arrived to examine the crime scene.

gloss.en: giving people good ideas or confidence
gloss.zh: 鼓舞人心的；启发灵感的

words.example: She gave an inspirational speech to the students.

gloss.en: to give someone the desire or confidence to act

words.example: The actors' enthusiasm inspired the kids.

gloss.en: to fix equipment in place so it can be used

words.example: They plan to install a new drainage system.

gloss.en: the act of fixing equipment in place

words.example: The price does not include installation costs.

gloss.en: a particular example or case of something

words.example: The report highlights several instances of injustice.

gloss.en: happening immediately

words.example: This account gives you instant access to your money.
